metadata.title: Identification of Mirror Repeats within the SWI 6 Gene of Saccharomyces cerevisiae
metadata.description: Bhardwaj, Vikash, Abstract, Namrata (2025): Identification of Mirror Repeats within the SWI 6 Gene of Saccharomyces cerevisiae. FishTaxa 37 (9): 157-163
references: 1. Altschul, S. F., Gish, W., Miller, W., Myers, E. W., & Lipman, D. J. (1990). Basic Local Alignment Search Tool. Journal of Molecular Biology, 215(3), 403-410. https://doi.org/10.1016/S0022-2836(05)80360-2
2. Bhardwaj, Vikash, Swapni, Gupta, Sitaram, Meena, & Kulbhushan, Sharma. (2013). FPCB: A simple and swift strategy for mirror repeat identification.
3. Elledge, S. J. (1996). Cell cycle checkpoints: Preventing an identity crisis. Science, 274(5293), 1029-1030. https://doi.org/10.1126/science.274.5293.1029
4. Hartwell, L. H., et al. (2001). The Saccharomyces cerevisiae cell cycle. Yeast, 18(12), 1145-1161. https://doi.org/10.1002/yea.703

5. Ho, Y., Costanzo, M., Moore, L., Kobayashi, R., & Andrews, B. J. (1999). Regulation of transcription at the Saccharomyces cerevisiae start transition by Stb1, a Swi6-binding protein. Molecular and Cellular Biology, 19(8), 5267-5278. https://doi.org/10.1128/MCB.19.8.5267

6. Horak, C. E., Luscombe, N. M., Qian, J., Bertone, P., Piccirrillo, S., Gerstein, M., & Snyder, M. (2002). Complex transcriptional circuitry at the G1/S transition in Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Genes & Development, 16(23), 3017-3033. https://doi.org/10.1101/gad.1039602
7. Lister, R., et al. (2011). Exploring the role of mirror repeats in genetic instability. Journal of Molecular Biology, 408(1), 32- 41. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jmb.2011.03.017

8. Mittal, P., & Roberts, C. W. M. (2020). The SWI/SNF complex in cancer - biology, biomarkers and therapy. Nature Reviews. Clinical Oncology, 17(7), 435-448. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41571-020-0357-3

9. Pearson, W. R. (1994). Using the FASTA search algorithm to compare protein and nucleotide sequences. In Methods in Enzymology (Vol. 183, pp. 63-98). Academic Press. https://doi.org/10.1016/0076-6879(90)83005-L

10. Sidorova, J. M., Mikesell, G. E., & Breeden, L. L. (1995). Cell cycle-regulated phosphorylation of Swi6 controls its nuclear localization. Molecular Biology of the Cell, 6(12), 1641-1658. https://doi.org/10.1091/mbc.6.12.1641
11. Tyers, M., & Jorgensen, P. (2000). Proteolysis and the control of cell cycle transitions. Nature Reviews Molecular Cell Biology, 1(3), 141-149. https://doi.org/10.1038/35040594
12. Urrutia, A. O., & Hurst, L. D. (2003). The evolution of the mirror repeat: Adaptive and nonadaptive perspectives. Trends in Genetics, 19(9), 414-419. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.tig.2003.06.005
13. Wright, F. (1999). The mirror-repeat method of identifying regulatory sequences in the genome. Journal of Molecular Biology, 291(1), 31-39. https://doi.org/10.1006/jmbi.1999.2831

14. Yadav, P., Kumari, J., Yadav, P., Yadav, R., Yadav, S., Sharma, D., Singh, A., Sehrawat, B., Yadav, M., & Yadav, S. (2024). Identification of mirror repeats in viral genomes using FPCB analysis: Life sciences-bioinformatics for health care. International Journal of Life Science and Pharma Research, 14(4), L12-L19. https://doi.org/10.22376/ijlpr.2024.14.4.L12-L19

15. Zhao, J., Xie, X., & Yu, H. (2020). Identification and functional prediction of mirror repeats in yeast using bioinformatics tools. BMC Genomics, 21(1), 112. https://doi.org/10.1186/s12864-020-6530-4

16. Zhang, Z., Schwartz, S., Wagner, L., & Miller, W. (2000). A greedy algorithm for aligning DNA sequences. Journal of Computational Biology, 7(1-2), 203-214. https://doi.org/10.1089/10665270050081478

data.title: Identification of Mirror Repeats within the SWI 6 Gene of Saccharomyces cerevisiae

metadata.title: juangancio/eeg-spatial-analysis: Supporting code for "Permutation entropy analysis of EEG signals for distinguishing eyes-open and eyes-closed brain states: Comparison of different approaches"
metadata.description: Python scrips for the spatial analysis of EEG signals, as well as MATLAB scripts for plotting figures These scrips reproduce the analysis performed and published in the work: "Permutation entropy analysis of EEG signals for distinguishing eyes-open and eyes-closed brain states: Comparison of different approaches" (https://doi.org/10.1063/5.0200029).

data.title: juangancio/eeg-spatial-analysis: Supporting code for "Permutation entropy analysis of EEG signals for distinguishing eyes-open and eyes-closed brain states: Comparison of different approaches"

metadata.title: Flowchart of a pre-processing pipeline for preparing lumbar spine MRI volumes for voxel-wise analysis
metadata.description: Overview of a pre-processing pipeline for preparing lumbar spine MR volumes for direct inter-subject voxel-wise comparison. Signal intensities and morphology are normalized against a reference subject.

creators.name: Hermansen, Erland
creators.affiliation: Norwegian University of Science and Technology

data.title: Flowchart of a pre-processing pipeline for preparing lumbar spine MRI volumes for voxel-wise analysis

metadata.title: Fig. 12 in Introduction Of Green Economy-Based Bio-Floc Technology for Sustainable Fish Production in Some Drier Regions of Ganjam Districts of Odisha
metadata.description: Fig. 12 Box plot for each treatment of character of profit The ANOVA results indicate that there is a no significant difference among the treatment means hence multiple comparison cannot be performed (Table 15,16 and Fig 11).

These files corresponds to qPCR data and scripts for statistical analysis for the paper from Bonnamy et al  entitled:

Small impact of the host plant physiological state on the genome formula of a nanovirus

creators.affiliation: Maladies Infectieuses et Vecteurs: Écologie, Génétique, Évolution et Contrôle

metadata.title: SCIENCE BFSP CoS | WP2 | D2.4: Connecting and sharing pan-genome information across different plant platforms
metadata.description: This deliverable (D2.4) reports on the prototyping of data formats, development of API calls, and the subsequent integration and visualization of pan-genome data across established plant platforms. Led by ELIXIR UK with assistance from ELIXIR Belgium, this work was conducted under Work Package 3 (WP3) of the E-PAN project within the BFSP Commissioned Service, specifically linking the PLAZA/FRINGE comparative genomics environment with the KnetMiner knowledge graph.

contributors.name: Heuermann, Marc Christian
contributors.affiliation: Leibniz Institute of Plant Genetics and Crop Plant Research

grants.title: European Life-science Infrastructure for Biological Information

data.title: SCIENCE BFSP CoS | WP2 | D2.4: Connecting and sharing pan-genome information across different plant platforms
